title: Injured hiker rescued from White Mountains via helicopter
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A solo hiker was rescued by helicopter from the Bondcliff Trail in Lincoln, New Hampshire, after injuring his leg.
The hiker, a man in his 60s from Plymouth, N.H., texted 911 and was located 7.5 miles from the trailhead.
The NH Army National Guard helicopter extracted him and took him to Dartmouth Hitchcock Medical Center for treatment.

title: State trooper hospitalized after crash with alleged wrong-way driver in Peabody
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A state trooper was hospitalized after a wrong-way crash on Route 1 in Peabody early Sunday.
The driver, Lucas DeAlmeida Benedetto, faces charges including operating under the influence and negligent operation.
Massachusetts Senate proposed infrastructure upgrades to prevent wrong-way driving after a similar fatal crash in May.

title: ‘A surreal experience’: Readers react to loud boom in Mass. caused by meteor
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A sonic boom was heard across eastern Massachusetts, southern New Hampshire, and parts of Rhode Island and Connecticut on Saturday.
Meteorologists confirmed a bolide meteor entry caused the boom, which was felt and heard by 89% of 285 respondents.
Residents reported shaking houses, startling pets, and initial fears of explosions or earthquakes before learning it was a meteor.

title: Mass. town warns of ‘neurological symptoms’ spotted in animals
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Athol sees increase in wild animals with neurological symptoms like stumbling and seizures, prompting a town advisory.
Affected animals mainly found in downtown Athol, Pinedale, and Pequig neighborhoods, animal control reports.
Residents urged to vaccinate pets and report any wildlife displaying odd behavior to Athol police.

title: ‘It’s a special feeling’: Mickey Gasper never gave up on his Major League dream, and now he’s reaping the rewards
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Mickey Gasper, a former Merrimack High player, is now hitting .333 for the Boston Red Sox this season.
Gasper made his Major League debut with the Red Sox in August 2024 after six years in the Minors.
The 30-year-old switch-hitter can play multiple positions, offering versatility and a boost to the Red Sox's offense.

title: Mass. family, Worcester woman identified as victims in deadly Virginia bus crash
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Greenfield family and a Worcester woman were victims in a Virginia crash involving a bus on May 29.
Dmitri Doncev and his family were traveling to South Carolina when their car was hit and caught fire.
Bus driver Jing S. Dong faces charges of involuntary manslaughter after failing to slow down for traffic.

title: Connor Wong’s 3-RBI night helped power Red Sox win, and it couldn’t come at a better time for him individually
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Connor Wong delivered 3 RBIs in the Red Sox's recent win over Cleveland.
Wong, once a starting catcher, has seen reduced play due to emergence of Carlos Narvaez and Mickey Gasper.
Despite losing primary catching duties, Wong's performance may bolster his position as the secondary catcher.

title: Sonny Gray continues to be great addition to Red Sox rotation, earns 6th win vs. Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray pitched six innings with seven strikeouts, leading the Red Sox to a 9-1 win over the Guardians.
Gray's May ERA was 2.00 over five starts, contributing to Boston's 7-3 record in his appearances.
Gray has a 3.06 season ERA and earns $31 million with a mutual option for 2027 in his contract.

title: Here’s the full lineup of athletes and celebrities involved in Drake Maye’s charity softball game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Drake Maye hosts charity softball game at Polar Park in Worcester on Sunday afternoon.
Participants include Patriots coaches Mike Vrabel and Josh McDaniels, Julian Edelman, and Zdeno Chara.
Event supports MayeDay foundation for children and families facing hardship.

title: Potential A.J. Brown Patriots-Eagles trade reportedly has ‘framework in place’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots have framework for A.J. Brown trade with Eagles, contingent on post-June 1 salary cap benefits for Eagles.
Eagles seek first-round pick from Patriots, who are hesitant, per NFL Network's Ian Rapoport report.
Patriots are possible destination for Brown to bolster roster after Stefon Diggs' departure, amid ongoing trade talks.
